heading: Sustainment reviews
label: 4323 Sustainment reviews
notes: Amendments2025— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section required a sustainment review of each covered system not later than five years after declaration of initial operational capability of a major defense acquisition program, and every five years thereafter throughout the life cycle of the covered system.Pub. L. 119–602021—, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 116–283, § 1848(c)section 2441 of this titleSubsec. (a). , in first sentence, substituted “covered system” for “major weapon system”, “, and every five years thereafter throughout the life cycle of the covered system,” for “and throughout the life cycle of the weapon system”, and “costs of the covered system” for “costs of the weapon system” and struck out second sentence which read as follows: “For any review after the first one, the Secretary concerned shall use availability and reliability thresholds and cost estimates as the basis for the circumstances that prompt such a review.”Pub. L. 116–283, § 802(c)(1)Subsec. (b). , inserted “assess execution of the life cycle sustainment plan of the covered system and” before “include the following elements:” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 116–283, § 802(c)(2)(A)Subsec. (b)(10). , added par. (10).Pub. L. 116–283, § 802(c)(2)(B)Subsec. (c). , substituted “sections 4324 and 4325” for “sections 2337 and 2337a”.Pub. L. 116–283, § 1848(c)Subsec. (d). , added subsec. (d).Pub. L. 116–283, § 802(c)(3)Subsec. (e). , added subsec. (e).Pub. L. 116–283, § 802(c)(3)Subsec. (e)(1). , substituted “section 4324” for “section 2337”.Pub. L. 116–283, § 1883(b)(2)Subsec. (e)(2)(B). , substituted “section 4214(d)” for “section 2435(d)”.Pub. L. 116–283, § 1883(b)(2)2017—Subsec. (a). , inserted at end “The Secretary concerned shall make the memorandum and supporting documentation for each sustainment review available to the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment within 30 days after the review is completed.”Pub. L. 115–91, § 816Subsec. (c). , substituted “sections 2337 and 2337a of this title” for “ and section 832 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2012 (; note)”.Pub. L. 115–91, § 836(b)(2)section 2337 of this titlePublic Law 112–8110 U.S.C. 2430

Effective Date of 2021 AmendmentAmendment by sections 1848(c) and 1883(b)(2) of effective , with additional provisions for delayed implementation and applicability of existing law, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. children.text: identify any factors contributing to the failure of a major weapon system to meet such requirements and objectives;
develop and implement a corrective action plan to address such factors in an expeditious manner; and
inform the submission of materials to Congress required by and the development of the future-years defense program required by .section 118(c)(2) of this titlesection 221 of this title
Findings from a review of the effectiveness of the life-cycle sustainment plan required under for a major weapon system.section 4324 of this title

A description of how such assessments informed the submission of materials to Congress required by and the development of the future-years defense program required by .section 118(c)(2) of this titlesection 221 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: an identification of factors contributing to such failure;

children.label: B
children.text: a mitigation plan to address supply, maintenance, or other issues contributing to such failure; and

children.label: C
children.text: a description of any corrective action plan required by subsection (a)(2) and an update on progress made in implementing such a plan.

A summary of actions taken by the Secretary to ensure that each covered system of the military department under the jurisdiction of the Secretary meets the applicable operational readiness requirements and materiel readiness objectives in the most cost-effective manner practicable.

children.heading: Covered System Defined.—
children.label: c Covered System Defined.—
children.text: In this section, the term “covered system” has the meaning given in .section 4324 of this title
